title: acer negundo subsp. interior
intro: Acer negundo subsp. interior, commonly known as the Interior Box Elder, is a subspecies of the Box Elder tree, native to North America. The plant's inner bark contains a compound called phytosterol, which has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The medicinal properties of Acer negundo subsp. interior are believed to help with wound healing, reducing fever, and alleviating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of the plant, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, are used to treat various health conditions, and it is also used as a natural remedy for skin conditions, such as acne and eczema.
